behavior_summary: CATAPORT::hatch handles the game event where an egg object hatches: it checks preconditions, hides the screen, teleports the hatched NPC to a destination determined by the egg type, then restores the screen.
The handler first aborts if the global flag postEarthQuake is set (preventing hatching during/post-earthquake).
It compares two Z (height) values obtained from the egg (Item::getZ called twice) and only proceeds if the values differ (guarding against an unwanted vertical condition).

If guards pass, it calls a process FadeToBlack(pid), suspends to let the fade complete, queries the egg type via Egg::getEggId, obtains a map id via Npc::getMap, teleports the resulting NPC via Npc::teleport to one of two hard-coded coordinate pairs depending on egg id, then calls process FadeFromBlack(pid) and suspends again to complete the transition.
notable_control_flow: Guard: read of global postEarthQuake -> immediate return if true (prevents U8P_CATAPORT::hatch).
Guard: two calls to Item::getZ on the egg -> if Z values are equal the function returns early (only continue when vertical positions differ).

Visual-suspension sequence: FadeToBlack(pid) is called and the process suspends/waits for completion before teleport; similarly FadeFromBlack(pid) is called and the process suspends again after teleport.
Branching on egg type: Egg::getEggId() selects between two teleport coordinate pairs passed to Npc::teleport.
Map id is dynamically retrieved from the egg/NPC with Npc::getMap and passed into the teleport call.
open_questions: Why are two Item::getZ() values compared on the same egg? (appears to be a vertical-state guard — exact intent is inferred).

Which exact entity is being teleported after hatching (the egg object, an associated NPC spawn, or another linked instance)? The code teleports an NPC-like object using Npc::teleport but the identity is inferred.
What are the semantic meanings of the two hard-coded destination coordinate pairs (are they staging positions, different nests, quest-specific locations)?
Are there additional side effects (inventory, quest progression, object destruction) performed elsewhere when an egg hatches that this handler relies on?

main_function.name: U8P_CATAPORT::hatch
main_function.purpose: Handle an egg hatching event: ensure preconditions are met, perform a fade-to-black visual transition, teleport the hatched NPC to a destination chosen by egg type, then fade back in.
entry_conditions: Triggered on the egg U8P_CATAPORT::hatch event (Event 7).
Only executes when global flag postEarthQuake is false.
Only continues when two Item::getZ() results differ (inferred vertical-condition guard).

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Query an object's Z/elevation coordinate; used as a guard.

function_map.name: U8P_CATAPORT::process FadeToBlack
function_map.role: Start a fade-to-black for the calling process (visual hide before teleport).

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Return egg type identifier to choose teleport destination.

function_map.name: Npc::getMap
function_map.role: Retrieve map id for the NPC/egg to pass into teleport.

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Move an NPC to specified map coordinates; used to place the hatched creature.

function_map.name: U8P_CATAPORT::process FadeFromBlack
function_map.role: End the fade-to-black (fade back in) for the calling process.

external_calls.purpose: Read egg's Z/elevation twice to compare and gate hatching on a vertical condition.
Visually hide the scene before teleporting the hatched entity; the process suspends to wait for completion.
Determine egg type / U8P_CATAPORT::hatch outcome to select teleport destination.
Obtain the map id for the teleport destination arguments.
Teleport the hatched NPC to a coordinate pair chosen by egg id on the obtained map.
Reveal the scene after teleport; the process suspends to wait for completion.

reads.name: postEarthQuake
reads.likely_effect: When true, prevents CATAPORT::hatch from running (hatching disabled during/post earthquake).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: CATAPORT::hatch handles the game event where an egg object hatches: it checks preconditions, hides the screen, teleports the hatched NPC to a destination determined by the egg type, then restores the screen.
